objective: Close the documented gaps between the current session detail implementation and the session dossier expectations defined in product and roadmap docs.

ssot_sources.role: product expectation for landing and session detail behavior
next-step roadmap for evolving detail page into an evidence-centered dossier

states_explainer.TODO: Gap is acknowledged but implementation has not started.
states_explainer.DOING: Work is active across code, payload, or tests.
states_explainer.DONE: Visible behavior, payload shape, and tests are aligned.

tasks.title: Add Future Actions section with safe placeholders
details: Add a dedicated Future Actions block to the session detail page.
Start with non-destructive placeholders only: Ask This Session and Continue / Resume Session.
Make the UI clearly present these as roadmap placeholders, not active workflows.

context.why: The roadmap names this as the immediate implementation step and the product expectation requires a future-actions layer on the dossier page.
context.current_gap: The current detail page ends with evidence blocks and timeline; there is no action surface yet.

acceptance_criteria: Detail page shows a Future Actions section below the timeline or in the lower action area.
Two placeholder cards are visible: Ask This Session and Continue / Resume Session.
Copy makes clear that the actions are not yet wired to destructive runtime behavior.
Playwright coverage checks the section on a published or mocked detail route.

tasks.title: Make the time window explicit in payload and UI
details: Expose the full time window in the session artifact payload, including explicit end time.
Render a dedicated time-window block that shows started_at, ended_at, duration, and what evidence is scoped to that window.
Keep the session JSON or JSONL artifact as the source of truth for these timestamps.

context.why: The docs treat the session time window as one of the main coordinate layers for commits, files, timeline, and future query features.
context.current_gap: The backend calculates ended_at internally but does not expose it in the payload; the frontend shows duration and modified time but not the complete window.

acceptance_criteria: Session artifact payload contains explicit start, end, and duration fields needed by the UI.
Detail page shows when the session started, when it ended, and how long it ran.
The page copy explains that commits, files, and timeline are interpreted inside that window.
Backend unit tests and frontend/e2e tests cover the new time-window behavior.

tasks.title: Introduce session state model and action safety modes
details: Represent session operational state explicitly with labels such as archived, restorable, live, and queryable.
Represent action safety modes such as read-only, ask-only, and resume-allowed.
Use these states to control how Future Actions are presented and what they promise.

context.why: The product expectation requires action blocks to be honest about what is safe and what is actually available.
context.current_gap: The first state model shipped, but the published audit on 2026-03-12 showed that idle sessions can still render as live and sparse dossiers do not clearly communicate when evidence is thin.

acceptance_criteria: Session detail payload exposes a clear state or capability model for the page to render.
Future Actions cards degrade safely when the system is read-only or ask-only.
The UI does not imply resume support when the harness workflow is not implemented.

tasks.title: Add Topic Threads as a separate semantic layer
details: Add a topic_threads layer that summarizes what the session was actually about.
Keep this distinct from user intent evolution so direction and subject matter do not collapse into one list.
Show topic threads near the lower evidence/action area.

context.why: The docs explicitly distinguish intent from topics and use topic threads as the next semantic layer after current intent evolution.
context.current_gap: The payload and UI include intent_evolution but no topic-oriented layer.

acceptance_criteria: Session detail payload can carry topic_threads independently from intent_evolution.
The UI renders a visible Topic Threads block.
Topic labels are short, readable, and do not simply duplicate intent bullets.

tasks.title: Build an evidence matrix that aligns narrative and repository signals
details: Create a matrix or aligned multi-column block for user direction, commits, files, and timeline.
Make it easy for the eye to compare what the user asked for with what reached the repository.
Prefer a layout that reads as one story rather than separate independent cards.

context.why: The roadmap calls for a shift from isolated cards to an evidence-centered dossier with strong signal comparison.
context.current_gap: Current detail page now has honest state and sparse-evidence cues, but the published audit on 2026-03-12 still shows commits, files, timeline, and user direction as separate blocks with weak narrative linkage.

acceptance_criteria: The detail page includes a side-by-side or aligned evidence matrix view.
The matrix includes at least user direction, commits, files, and timeline.
A reviewer can visually compare conversation intent with repository outcomes without scanning four disconnected sections.

tasks.title: Connect commit narrative to modified files
details: Link commits and file changes instead of rendering them as unrelated lists.
At minimum, allow the detail page to show which files are plausibly associated with the commit narrative inside the same session window.
Preserve the fact that mismatch between intent and repository output is informative, not automatically an error.

context.why: The roadmap explicitly says the page should connect commits with files modified and the product doc treats commits as a meaningful evidence layer.
context.current_gap: The backend exposes git_commits and files_modified, but the published audit on 2026-03-12 still shows them as disconnected evidence instead of a grouped narrative with explicit linkage confidence.

acceptance_criteria: Commits and files are visually or structurally connected in the session detail view.
The page helps explain what likely reached the repo during the session window.
When linkage is uncertain, the UI states that clearly instead of overclaiming.

tasks.title: Show evidence priority and disagreement explicitly
details: Make the difference between source artifact, timeline, repository signals, and derived layers visible.
Label derived content so it does not silently outrank source evidence.
When intent and commit narrative diverge, preserve that divergence as useful signal.

context.why: The product doc defines an evidence priority rule and explicitly says the system should show disagreement instead of hiding it.
context.current_gap: Current page renders intent, commits, and timeline with similar presentation weight and without a trust hierarchy.

acceptance_criteria: Derived layers are visually marked as derived.
Source and timeline evidence retain higher trust presentation than summaries.
The page can show a mismatch between user direction and repository outcome without treating it as a bug by default.

tasks.title: Prepare real ask and resume flows behind the placeholders
details: Implement the first real headless question flow over a session artifact.
Implement a real continue or resume flow only when harness-specific safety and state rules are available.
Keep the original JSON artifact untouched and operate through a separate query or runtime layer.

context.why: The page should eventually become an operational hub, not only an archive.
context.current_gap: The ask-only query flow is now real and evidence-backed; resume remains intentionally safety-gated until a harness-specific continue workflow exists.

acceptance_criteria: Ask flow can answer a session question without modifying the source artifact.
Resume flow is gated by explicit safety and capability checks.
The UI distinguishes between available and unavailable operational actions.

tasks.title: Expand verification for visible dossier behaviors
details: Add or update backend tests for payload shape changes.
Add or update frontend and Playwright tests for new visible dossier sections and state cues.
Use published verification for any major visible behavior change.

context.why: The repo quality bar and memory cards require tests and published verification for visible UI, routing, auth, and payload changes.
context.current_gap: Coverage was expanded earlier, but the 2026-03-12 published audit revealed missing assertions for honest state labeling and sparse-evidence cues on real detail pages.

acceptance_criteria: Every new visible dossier section has test coverage.
Payload changes are covered by backend tests.
Major UI changes are verified against the published URL before closure.
